LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-04-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION GRANTED PER DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE SET FOR HEARING ON FEBRUARY 26, 2021 ARE OFF CALENDAR. IF DEFENDANT WISHES TO DEMUR OR MOVE TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IT SHALL COMPLY WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 430.41 AND 435.5. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Sep-03-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Patrina Harrison (415-567-8182; harrisonempa@gmail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
David Adams, Esq. (415-500-7700; dadams@blaxterlaw.com) of Blaxter Blackman LLP for Defendant.

Discussed (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC'S Motion To Stay this action pending resolution of the federal case involving the same incident and subject matter is granted. The stay will conserve court resources and avoid conflicts between state and federal courts. (Farmland Irr. Co. v. Dopplmaier (1957) 48 Cal.2d 208, 215; Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Patrina Harrison's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion granted per defendant's notice of non-opposition. Defendant's demurrer and motion to strike set for hearing on February 26, 2021 are off calendar. If defendant wishes to demur or move to strike the first amended complaint, it shall comply with Code of Civil Procedure sections 430.41 and 435.5.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
